message:
perf: VDBE row-cache, prepared-statement benchmarks, keyword fast-path, and metadata caching

A cohesive set of performance improvements across the hot execution path,
reducing redundant work in parsing, schema lookups, VDBE column reads,
and benchmark overhead.

VDBE per-position row cache (btree cursor + engine):
- Add BtCursor::position_stamp() returning (page_no, cell_idx) to identify
  the cursor's current leaf cell without payload decode
- Add StorageCursor::row_vals_buf to cache the fully-parsed record columns
  at a given position stamp, avoiding repeated parse_record_column() calls
  when multiple Column opcodes read from the same row
- Invalidate the row cache (row_vals_buf + last_position_stamp) on every
  mutation: Delete, Insert, IndexInsert, and conflict-resolution paths
- Refactor payload_includes_rowid_alias() to accept pre-parsed &[SqliteValue]
  instead of raw &[u8], eliminating a redundant per-column re-parse
- Add test_delete_invalidates_cached_storage_row_before_successor_column
  to verify cache correctness after delete-then-read sequences

Table execution metadata cache (connection):
- Introduce TableExecutionMetadataCacheEntry caching autoincrement table
  names, rowid alias columns, table column counts, and index DESC flags
  by root page, keyed on schema_generation
- Consolidate four separate schema-scanning methods into a single
  table_execution_metadata() builder with generation-based invalidation
- Invalidate on schema cookie change and DDL (CREATE/DROP/ALTER)
- Intentionally leave dynamic values (sqlite_sequence high-water marks,
  evaluated column defaults) uncached for correctness
- Add two tests: DDL invalidation and autoincrement live-value verification

Prepared-statement benchmarks:
- Convert all fsqlite benchmark hot loops from conn.query()/conn.execute()
  with format!() strings to conn.prepare() + stmt.query_with_params() /
  stmt.execute_with_params() with SqliteValue parameters
- Affects mixed_oltp_bench, operation_baseline_bench, read_heavy_bench,
  and comprehensive_bench — now benchmarks measure execution, not parsing

IN-subquery rewrite Cow optimization (connection):
- Change rewrite_in_subqueries_select() to return Cow<SelectStatement>,
  borrowing the original when no rewritable subquery is present
- Add select_contains_rewritable_subquery() fast-path check to skip
  cloning entirely for the common case of simple SELECTs
- Update all 10+ call sites to use rewritten.as_ref() / Cow::Owned
- Add two unit tests verifying Borrowed vs Owned return paths

Parser keyword fast-path (token.rs):
- Add lookup_common_keyword_bytes() matching ~50 frequent SQL keywords
  directly on &[u8] slices organized by length, avoiding the String
  allocation and to_ascii_uppercase() in the hot tokenizer path
- Wire into lookup_keyword() as an early-return before the existing match

MVCC simplification (begin_concurrent):
- Remove concurrent_write_page_with() lazy-builder variant; callers now
  pre-build PageData before acquiring the page lock
- Update SharedTxnPageIo to clone PageData outside the lock-acquisition
  loop, simplifying the retry path
- Remove the associated test for busy-path payload skip

Realdb E2E hardening:
- Add make_path_writable() helper and call it after copying database
  files and sidecars, fixing test failures on read-only source databases
- Properly handle Unix vs non-Unix permission APIs

Tracing allocation reduction (connection):
- Convert statement_sql from eager String to Option<String>, reusing
  the trace-registration copy for statement_reuse when both are needed

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
